mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 8% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 8%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 700k units signals weakening aggregate demand. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year training window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) index rising above 95 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as supply strength reduces cost pressures.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of instances when INDPRO > 95, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100k net new jobs per month combined with rising unemployment signals labor market inflection. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 100k AND UNRATE rising > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in 4-year rolling logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 230 combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals monetary tightening regime that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward earnings multiples, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expanding at annualized rate above 2% signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 650k units signals residential investment weakness that predicts broader demand contraction. This mechanism operates as a leading indicator of equity market weakness over 20 trading days as forward GDP growth expectations decline.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of instances when HOUST < 650k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
